reviews.review_description: Even for a couple bucks, I was expecting more. When you complete the current content you’re informed to wait for more in the near future. Been waiting for a few months. Still nothing more.
For a mobile game, the graphics are good and the whole atmosphere is nicely achieved. So far it plays like an old-school adventure (not a bad thing). Certainly worth the cash.

Great presentation. 

Very immersive but on the Oculus Go it is indeed out of scale.

That didn't bother me at all but I could see how it could bother other players.

I'm not too far into it & so far I'm having much fun. Watch out for the floor traps!

I really like this game but I want the rest of the story. The farthest I get is blowing up the wall to release the girl. Then it said I completed episode one. Are you ever going to release episode two? I need  more  of the story. Getting bored with this.
Please fix cave glitch

developer_response.body: Hello turbojoe1970, thank you for your message. Rest assured that the update is being reviewed by Oculus Store and will soon be published for download. We are very sorry for inconvenience. Best regards! UPDATE: Current version of the game should not have the cave glitch. Please play and check it out! :-D

reviews.review_title: not groundbreaking,  but worth your time
reviews.review_description:
I wish customers would recognize that the GearVR is not the Rift, and reviews should recognize that information. It's like buying a Jetta and getting upset that it doesn't go as fast as a Ferrari. -- with that out of the way, this game is worth your time. The movement is awkward and out feels like rolling around on a Segway. The visuals have a real sense of presence, and the use of reflective surfaces goes a long way. I find the best vr to be from the little tiny features that don't blow your mind, but the things you hardly notice. -- however I have just entered the cave after you walk through the area with booby-traps on the ground, but when I start descending,  I'm not really descending but staying on a flat plane, and then flying over the whole island and eventually you breakout of the game and start flying through the assets. Is the a problem anyone else is having? I'd like too figure out if the issue is my phone,  or 
a development issue.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your comment teamNONIN. With respect to the movement speed, we will surely make some adjustments in the upcoming updates. Distorted Team Vescom Studios.

reviews.review_description:
I got down the elevator and then had to stop for air.

Seems good.  Might be too intense for me.

Free movement, hold to walk. Speeds up too fast, probably so you can run away quickly...  Which probably happens a lot. Feels a bit weird when its calm though.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your comment Anthonsh. With respect to the movement speed, we will surely make some adjustments in the upcoming updates. Distorted Team Vescom Studios.

reviews.review_description:
I use s8+(exynos)
i have sound issue
all game sound has crash? have noise 
really big noise....how can i fix it?

-----

i fix it

 change gaming mod

developer_response.body: Hi Hongsungchan, sorry for the inconvenience. Could you please let us know if your sound related issue continues via email: support@vescomstudios.com. Meanwhile, please make sure that You have the latest version of the Oculus VR software installed on your Samsung device and that it is not a matter of the Bluetooth communications with any of your audio devices. We will do our best to help you. Distorted Team Vescom Studios.

reviews.review_title: The best ive seen so far
reviews.review_description: The fact that you can walk in place instead of teleport to move plus the great graphics makes this very immereive. Fantastic. If you dont buy this you have failed in life

reviews.review_title: Best mobile VR horror game to date.
reviews.review_description:
The main thing I want to praise is the originality. This is not just another haunted mansion or hospital with the same few scares. The plot is extremely close to Amnesia: Dark Descent, but that's still less of a tropefest than other VR horror titles. The assets are diverse and appealing. The settings are varied. The game is longer than other mobile VR horror titles.

Beyond that, I want to praise the control options. This game supports gamepad locomotion. Many mobile VR horror titles don't bother. It also supports the gyro pointer and ofc the touchpad, though I doubt anybody would ever wish to control the game that way. 

The mechanics of the game are polished. It has a nice, immersive in world menu for settings, new game, continue, etc. which many horror titles lack (Allison's Diary). It feels like a "real game" rather than a throw away 5 minute experience. I anticipate great things from this developer in the future.

Fun game so far but when I get to the cave like area, for some reason when I pick up the torch I fall through the ground and can look up and see the layout of the building I was in. Then it's just complete darkness and I have to restart the game. Haven't tried re-installing to see if it corrects the problem yet though.
Problem I had is now fixed
Really enjoyed this experience.
Best game i've played so far! The puzzles are on the light side but the graphics are top notch.  Oh, and the  spear in the gut made my week!

This game has potential to be great, but it’s not.

The controls with the Oculus Go controller don’t feel as natural as I would like. You can only move in the direction you are facing. There is no ability to strafe or walk backwards. Also, you can only run. When you run down stairs you are basically just jumping to the bottom of the steps. The pointer looks like a fencing sword and is a little distracting from immersion. Perhaps this is better played with a game pad. 

The game is short and ends without resolution. There seems to be a good plot developing. I’m not sure if this is Intended to be a demo of a full title or if the developer is going to release multiple installments that have to be purchased.

The visuals are good.  I liked the monster that you encounter. The floor traps caught me off guard the first time I stepped on them.  The atmosphere is creepy and makes you feel like something is always lurking around the corner.

developer_response.body: Hello and thank you for playing! We're constantly working with Oculus on optimizing controls. Unfortunately developer versions of the hardware are slightly different than consumer versions, and we still noticing some inequalities. Our game is our game is the first episode of more to come, and we are currently working on the second part.  Stay tuned for updates! Best regards, Kasia from Vescom Studios

reviews.review_description: This is one of the best Mobil VR games out there. No glitches, smooth through out the game. Highly recommend it.

reviews.review_title: performance is so bad on s7
reviews.review_description:
couldn't enjoy it. you get sick because framerate is dropping on my phone. i stopped playing because of this. comfort is very important in vr.

I am not very sensitive to motion sickness, but this was way to bad. maybe you should update the app for weaker phones than s8! thanks

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear that. We have optimized the game with our Samsung S7 as our main benchmark device. However, playing Distorted right after other visually complex games were device was already overheated could potentially influence your experience. Please bear in mind that some of the apps from a gallery (which is a separate category in store) are not as strictly checked for overheating which is probably the issue in this case. Please check if the problem occurs every time you play. We are very sorry for the inconvenience. Best regards!

reviews.review_description: Everything is too big on Oculus Go.
Cant wait for part 2

Me and my dad love puzzles, horror, and escape games, so this was exciting and interesting. But it was too easy. There was only one puzzle to solve (the wheels), but that took all but two seconds to figure out. You only have like two confrontations with the monster (which I was grateful for, since I'm a baby), and there wasn't much finding and exploring to do. It was an okay game but only took 20 mins for us to do, and I kind of expected more from an $8 game. Besides that, it's a great game.
